title: Nashua, N.H. man arrested after allegedly assaulting 3-month-old son
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Drew Babcock, 36, was arrested for allegedly assaulting his 3-month-old son in Nashua, N.H., causing serious injuries.
Babcock is charged with eight counts of first-degree domestic assault and is held without bail before his arraignment.
The child suffered from fractured ribs, femurs, and clavicle; an investigation was launched after a state report indicated serious injuries.

title: Here’s how much a remote per diem worker makes north of Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Francine, a 66-year-old senior project manager in clinical research, earns $27,000 annually working remotely per diem.
She shifted from full-time work in Boston to remote per diem work post-2023, citing health challenges and personal preferences.
Her biggest expense is healthcare since starting Medicare; she supplements her income with a pension and a 403(b).

title: NTSB: Flight instructor in fatal Mass. plane crash had ‘do not fly’ meds in system
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The NTSB reported William Hampton, a flight instructor in a 2024 Massachusetts plane crash, had 'do not fly' medications in his system.
Hampton had alprazolam, amitriptyline, zolpidem, and citalopram in his system, which may have impaired his ability to control the plane.
The crash killed Fredrika Ballard, Chad Davidson, and William Hampton; NTSB cited a stall and spin as probable causes of the crash.

title: WATCH: Bagpiper performs on St. Patrick’s Day in Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Bagpiper Nate Silva performed at Harry's Bar and Grill in Boston on St. Patrick's Day.
Silva played at over 30 venues during St. Patrick's Day celebrations, including multiple stops at Harry's network of bars.
With a repertoire of 100 annual events, Silva has been playing Scottish bagpipes since age 11.

title: 50 ways to make the most of spring in Massachusetts
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: DiParma Italian Table in West Yarmouth, Cape Cod, closed after over 20 years; a new restaurant opens in April.
Boston hotels and Airbnb increase rates for the summer's FIFA World Cup, offering bonuses for new hosts.
JetBlue canceled the seasonal Boston-Asheville route; affected customers to receive full refunds.

title: Tom Brady brought jokes, nostalgia, and a comeback tease to Jimmy Fallon ahead of flag football debut
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tom Brady discussed his first field return post-retirement on 'The Tonight Show,' previewing the Fanatics Flag Football Classic.
Brady shared his changed perspective on football due to his FOX broadcasting role and praised the next generation of players.
The flag football event, featuring Brady and Rob Gronkowski, was relocated to Los Angeles amid Middle Eastern conflicts.

title: This is the current state of the TSA at Logan Airport
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Over 30 TSA employees at Logan Airport have quit amid the ongoing government shutdown affecting paychecks.
Logan Airport has not yet experienced significant delays, but concerns rise as more TSA agents consider leaving.
Massport is testing a new camera system to display accurate TSA line wait times at Logan Airport terminals.

title: Man found dead in Brockton was in fight, then run over, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Brockton man Lianes Brandao, 28, died after a fight and hit-and-run on early Sunday near Cardoso Cafe.
Surveillance video shows Brandao in a fight before being run over by a gray 2013 Jeep Compass.
The vehicle operator was interviewed by police; Massachusetts State Police and Brockton police are investigating.

title: Pregnant, battling cancer — now she’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Jennifer Capone Haberek, 36, from Newton, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon after a cancer diagnosis during pregnancy.
Haberek was diagnosed with triple-negative breast cancer in 2024, while pregnant and with a 10-month-old son.
Running to support the Ellie Fund, Haberek celebrates two years cancer-free after overcoming a difficult treatment period.

title: A Korean trio of restaurants heads to Allston, starting with Sanbada
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Sanbada, part of a Korean restaurant trio, has opened in Allston, offering grilled fish sets and banchan.
Bombbada, a late-night eatery, will open in July, providing trendy dishes and a focus on soju and beer.
Jinjee, specializing in Korean BBQ, is scheduled to open in mid-April with tabletop grills and a traditional meal feel.
